title: Meckel Diverticulum: A Systematic Approach
objectives: Understand the pathophysiology of Meckel Diverticulum
Identify key diagnostic features of Meckel Diverticulum
Apply clinical reasoning to Meckel Diverticulum management
Recognize common pitfalls in Meckel Diverticulum

sections.title: Overview of Meckel Diverticulum
sections.content:
**Meckel Diverticulum**

Vitelline duct remnant, rule of 2s, ectopic gastric mucosa, Meckel scan, painless rectal bleeding

**Key Points:**
• Remnant of vitelline (omphalomesenteric) duct
• Rule of 2s: 2% prevalence, 2 feet from ileocecal valve, 2 inches long, 2 types of ectopic tissue (gastric/pancreatic), presents by age 2
• Most common cause of painless lower GI bleeding in children <2 years

sections.title: Pathophysiology & Key Features
sections.content:
• Remnant of vitelline (omphalomesenteric) duct
• Rule of 2s: 2% prevalence, 2 feet from ileocecal valve, 2 inches long, 2 types of ectopic tissue (gastric/pancreatic), presents by age 2

sections.title: Diagnosis & Management
sections.content:
• Most common cause of painless lower GI bleeding in children <2 years
• Ectopic gastric mucosa → acid secretion → ulceration → bleeding
• Diagnosis: Technetium-99m pertechnetate scan (Meckel scan) detects ectopic gastric mucosa

sections.title: Clinical Pearls
sections.content:
**High-Yield Points for Meckel Diverticulum:**

• Most common cause of painless lower GI bleeding in children <2 years
• Ectopic gastric mucosa → acid secretion → ulceration → bleeding
• Diagnosis: Technetium-99m pertechnetate scan (Meckel scan) detects ectopic gastric mucosa

mcqs.question: Which of the following is MOST characteristic of Meckel Diverticulum?

options.text: Requires invasive testing in all cases

mcqs.question: What is the KEY diagnostic or management consideration for Meckel Diverticulum?

options.text: Diagnosis: Technetium-99m pertechnetate scan (Meckel scan) detects ectopic gastric mucosa
options.explanation: Diagnosis: Technetium-99m pertechnetate scan (Meckel scan) detects ectopic gastric mucosa

options.text: Immediate surgical intervention in all cases

mcqs.question: A patient presents with findings consistent with Meckel Diverticulum. Which finding would you expect?

options.text: Rule of 2s: 2% prevalence, 2 feet from ileocecal valve, 2 inches long, 2 types of ectopic tissue (gastric/pancreatic), presents by age 2
